title: eupatorium agrigaudium
intro: Eupatorium agrigaudium, also known as Ague Tree or Boneset, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its tall stature and clusters of small white flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and ease respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as colds, flu, and arthritis. Eupatorium agrigaudium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, and is also used in topical applications for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.
